task_context.actionable_question: What are the strongest predictors of student success in this dataset?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Rank top 5 features by correlation with avg_score [FE]. Note dataset-specific features.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The analysis reveals a moderate positive correlation between active days and average score, indicating that increased engagement may enhance student performance. Conversely, a weak negative correlation exists with disadvantage score, suggesting that higher disadvantage scores are associated with lower average scores.

Insights: Active Days Correlation: The feature 'active_days' shows a moderate positive correlation (0.4181) with average score, indicating that students who engage more frequently tend to achieve higher scores. | Total Clicks Correlation: The feature 'total_clicks' has a moderate positive correlation (0.3237) with average score, suggesting that increased interaction with course materials may also contribute to better academic performance. | Disadvantage Score Correlation: The feature 'disadvantage_score' exhibits a weak negative correlation (-0.1353) with average score, indicating that higher disadvantage scores are linked to lower average scores.

Educational implications: Encouraging active participation and engagement in learning activities may lead to improved student outcomes.

Recommendations: high - Implement strategies to increase student engagement, such as gamification or interactive assignments. - Enhancing engagement can leverage the positive correlation with average scores. | medium - Provide additional support for students with high disadvantage scores. - Addressing the challenges faced by these students may help improve their academic performance.

explanation.summary: The analysis reveals a moderate positive correlation between active days and average score, indicating that increased engagement may enhance student performance. Conversely, a weak negative correlation exists with disadvantage score, suggesting that higher disadvantage scores are associated with lower average scores.

insights.title: Active Days Correlation
insights.description: The feature 'active_days' shows a moderate positive correlation (0.4181) with average score, indicating that students who engage more frequently tend to achieve higher scores.

evidence.context: This is the highest correlation in the dataset.

insights.title: Total Clicks Correlation
insights.description: The feature 'total_clicks' has a moderate positive correlation (0.3237) with average score, suggesting that increased interaction with course materials may also contribute to better academic performance.

evidence.context: This is the second highest correlation in the dataset.

insights.title: Disadvantage Score Correlation
insights.description: The feature 'disadvantage_score' exhibits a weak negative correlation (-0.1353) with average score, indicating that higher disadvantage scores are linked to lower average scores.

evidence.context: This correlation suggests potential challenges faced by students with higher disadvantage scores.

educational_implications: Encouraging active participation and engagement in learning activities may lead to improved student outcomes.

recommendations.action: Implement strategies to increase student engagement, such as gamification or interactive assignments.
recommendations.rationale: Enhancing engagement can leverage the positive correlation with average scores.

recommendations.action: Provide additional support for students with high disadvantage scores.
recommendations.rationale: Addressing the challenges faced by these students may help improve their academic performance.

confidence.reason: The dataset is robust with a sufficient number of samples for reliable correlation analysis.

required_core_outputs.description: Rank top 5 features by correlation with avg_score [FE].

evaluation_constraints.description: When ranked features include sensitive demographic, lifestyle, or socioeconomic attributes, identify their sensitivity and avoid framing correlation as prescriptive guidance.
Do not interpret feature correlation as causal importance.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Retained as applicable pending review because ranked factors may include sensitive attributes and may influence intervention policy.
